# agentic-toolkit consumer schema

<!-- DO NOT EDIT — generated by tools/schemagen from internal/stack and internal/lockfile struct definitions. Run `go generate ./...` to refresh. -->

A consumer repo opts into the toolkit by committing two files at the repo root:

- `.agentic-toolkit.yaml` — entry-point **stack manifest**: declares which other stacks to extend and which definitions to layer on top. Hand-edited.
- `.agentic-toolkit.lock.yaml` — pinned record of what the resolver actually fetched. Resolver-written; commit it.

## Stack manifest

**Path:** `.agentic-toolkit.yaml` at the repo root, or any `stacks/<name>.yaml` file in any repo published for sharing.

The same shape is used everywhere: the consumer's entry-point file is just a stack with no extra ceremony. There is no "preset" / "consumer config" distinction.

### Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `description` | `string` | no | One-line summary used by tooling when this stack is imported by another stack. |
| `root` | `string` | no | Convention root for bare-name lookups, relative to the stack file's repo root. Defaults to "definitions". |
| `extends` | `[]ExtendsRef` | no | Other stacks to layer under this one. Applied in declared order; later entries override earlier ones. Each entry is an external URL (with .git/ boundary) or a local path (./...). |
| `skills` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `agents` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `rules` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `instructions` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `commands` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `hooks` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `mcp` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |
| `settings` | `[]EntryRef` | no |  |

### Per-entry resolution

Each entry in `extends:` and in the per-category lists (`skills`, `agents`, `rules`, `instructions`, `commands`, `hooks`, `mcp`, `settings`) is a string that the parser disambiguates by shape:

- **External URL** — contains `.git/` as the boundary between repo URL and in-repo path. Optional `@<ref>` selects a git ref (branch, tag, sha). Example: `github.com/owner/repo.git/skills/foo@main`. The resolver fetches the repo and locates the bundle/file at the in-repo path.
- **Local path** — starts with `./` or `/`. Resolved relative to the directory holding the stack file itself. Example: `./team-skills/foo` in a stack at `repo/stacks/team.yaml` resolves to `repo/stacks/team-skills/foo`.
- **Bare name** — anything else. Resolved under `<root>/<plural>/<name>...` in the stack file's source FS. The default `root` is `definitions`; override it with the top-level `root:` field.

Bare names are not permitted in `extends:` — every extends entry must be either a URL or a `./path`.

### Override semantics

`extends:` is processed depth-first, post-order: each imported stack's entries apply before the importing stack's own entries, so the **importing** stack always wins on `(category, name)` collisions. Sibling extends apply in declared order, so later siblings override earlier ones. The entry-point file's own entries always win last.

### `Source` (URL extends entries)

Source identifies a fetchable git source. Currently only used internally by the resolver; consumers write URLs as plain strings inside per-category lists and `extends:`.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | `string` | **yes** |  |
| `ref` | `string` | no |  |

### Example

```yaml
extends:
  - github.com/pedromvgomes/agentic-toolkit.git/stacks/default.yaml@main
skills:
  - ./local-skills/internal-helper
  - github.com/some-team/their-skills.git/skills/lint-helper@v1
rules:
  - github.com/some-team/their-rules.git/rules/style.md@v1
```

## Lockfile

**Path:** `.agentic-toolkit.lock.yaml`

The resolver writes the lockfile after a successful sync. It pins every source the run touched — every URL reached via the entry-point stack's `extends:` graph plus every URL reached via per-category URL entries. The current schema version is **2**.

### Top-level fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `version` | `int` | **yes** | Lockfile schema version. Currently must be 1. |
| `sources` | `[]ResolvedSource` | **yes** | Every source the resolver touched, in deterministic order. |

### `sources` entry (`ResolvedSource`)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | `string` | **yes** | Repository URL (e.g. github.com/owner/repo). |
| `ref` | `string` | **yes** | Git ref the resolver was asked to fetch (branch, tag, or sha). The resolver records the default-branch name here when the consumer config left ref empty. |
| `sha` | `string` | **yes** | Concrete commit sha the ref pointed to at resolution time. |

### Example

```yaml
version: 2
sources:
  - url: github.com/pedromvgomes/agentic-toolkit
    ref: main
    sha: 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01234567
  - url: github.com/anthropics/skills
    ref: main
    sha: fedcba9876543210fedcba9876543210fedcba98
```
